title: App crashes on startup with SIGSEGV when GPU memory exceeds 8GB
body:
## Description

After the latest update (v2.4.1), the application consistently crashes on startup with a SIGSEGV error when the system GPU has more than 8GB of VRAM allocated by other processes.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open any other GPU-intensive application (e.g., a game or video editor)
2. Ensure GPU memory usage is above 8GB
3. Launch the app
4. App crashes immediately with SIGSEGV

## Expected Behavior

The app should either launch normally or display a meaningful error message about insufficient GPU memory.

## Actual Behavior

```
Process: myapp [1234]
Signal: SIGSEGV (Segmentation fault)
Thread 0 crashed with X86 Thread State (64-bit):
  rax: 0x0000000000000000
```

## Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 22.04
- GPU: NVIDIA RTX 4090 (24GB VRAM)
- Driver: 535.113.01
- App version: 2.4.1

## Additional Context

This worked fine in v2.4.0. Reverting to 2.4.0 fixes the issue.
